title: Braised Chuck Roast
description: A hearty, comforting dish of slow-cooked chuck roast in a rich, flavorful sauce.
instructions: Season the chuck roast with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the chuck roast until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the chuck roast from the pot and set aside.
Add the garlic, ginger, fish sauce, soy sauce, and brown sugar to the pot.
Stir to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the star anise and cinnamon sticks to the pot.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Return the chuck roast to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan.
Serve the braised chuck roast with the sauce spooned over the top.
